title: 2 Using the Payment Solutions Manual
body_text:
2	Using the Payment Solutions Manual
2.1	Overview of the Payment Solutions Manual
The Payment Solutions Manual is owned by Central 1âs Digital & Payment Services Department and is published by Central 1âs Product Compliance & Design Department.
The Payment Solutions Manual is available on the Forms & Manuals page of Central 1âs Client Centre. The manual includes the following areas:
How to Use this Manual
â Outlines how to use the manual, including a Copyright and Disclaimer that outlines allowable usage of materials in the manual as per the
Copyright Act
, and a Google Analytics Cookie Policy.
Releases
â Contains releases for the current year and the previous few years.
Document Library
â Lists all Payments forms referenced in the Payment Solutions Manual. The forms are organized into either National Forms or BC Only Forms. The introductory chapter of each Part also includes a Document Library section that lists all forms associated with that product or service. Forms are also available on the Forms & Manuals page of the Client Centre.
Parts
â Each product or service has its own Part and related Parts are grouped into Volumes. Each Part contains Chapters, and each Chapter contains Sections. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Structure of the Payment Solutions Manual
2.2	User Access
Most Client Centre users have automatic access to the Payment Solutions Manual. Special setup is not required. However, the Payment Solutions Manual contains links to Operations Manual Program content which is subscription based and only available to certain users at BC and Ontario credit unions (e.g., links to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part). Users who select a link to which access is restricted will receive an error message. Contact Central 1, Product Compliance & Design (
manuals@central1.com
), for more information or to inquire about subscription services. Non-subscribers may have access to similar content through their provincial central or through their own forms library.
2.3	Viewing Content
Navigating Content
Use the table of contents on the left to explore each Part of the Payment Solutions Manual. Select the folder icons to expand and collapse different areas. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Table of Contents
To show a wider view of the content, select the hamburger menu at the top of the page. This hides the table of contents. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Hamburger Menu
Use the arrow buttons on the right to move forward and backward between Sections. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Arrow Buttons
Viewing Multiple Sections
Keep multiple Sections of the manual open by right-clicking a Section in the table of contents and choosing
Open in new tab
or
Open in new window
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Open Multiple Screens
2.4	Searching
How to Search
Enter a search term or phrase in the box beside the magnifying glass icon. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Search Box
Once you type four or more characters, your results appear in real time and change as you type. You can still do searches for 1â3 characters (e.g., âAFTâ) but the real-time search feature wonât work. Press the
Enter
key
after entering 3 or less characters to see the search results.
You can also filter searches by Volume, either before or after performing the search. To do so, select the drop-down menu beside the search box and then select the applicable Volume(s). See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Filter Search Results
The results are sorted by which areas have the most instances of the search term, although only the first Section of each Chapter shows as the search result. The top of the page shows whether any filters were applied as well as how many search results were found. See Figure 8.
The search results provide a link that includes the Part name as well as the Chapter name and number. The Volume name is also visible on the bottom right of the result.
Figure 8: Search Results
Search Highlighting
The term will continue to be highlighted when you select a search result link. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Search Highlighting
To return to the search page, select the magnifying glass. Do not use the back arrow or your browserâs Back button.
Use quotation marks to search for phrases, e.g., âdishonoured chequeâ.
2.5	Printing
Print a Whole Chapter
To print a whole Chapter, select the Print icon, choose
All
, and select
Print
. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Print Chapter
Print a Selection of Text
Depending on your internet browser, you may print a selection of text. To print some of the text on a page, select the text to be printed, right-click on the selected text, select
Print
, ensure the âSelection onlyâ option is pre-selected, then select
Print
again. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Print Selection
Creating a PDF
You can create a PDF of any Part or Release in the manual. Select the
Print to PDF
option. Your PDF will open in a new tab. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Print to PDF
2.6	Emailing Feedback or Questions to Client Support Services
To send feedback or a question about a part of the Payment Solutions Manual to Central 1, Client Support Services, select the Email icon. This generates an email with a link to the Section of the manual that you are currently viewing. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Email Feedback
2.7	Getting Payment Solutions Manual Updates
Finding Releases
A Release is a publication that describes updates to a Part or a form in the
Payment Solutions Manual. Select
Releases
on the left to view a chronological list of Releases. See Figure 14.
Figure 14: Accessing Releases
Signing up for Client Centre News Notifications and Setting Client Centre News Update Preferences
Client Centre users are notified of updates to the Payment Solutions Manual via the news updates posted in the Latest Resources section on the Client Centreâs Forms & Manuals page. A news update typically provides a link to the related Release.
To sign up for news item updates and to set Client Centre news preferences:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 15.
Note: 	Youâll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itâs your first login to the Client Centre, youâve deleted your cookies, or youâre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 15: Client Centre Login
Select your name, then select
Subscribe to Email Notifications
. See Figure 16.
Figure 16: Subscribe to Email Notifications
Enter your first name, last name, and email address, then select the email notifications youâd like to receive. Select
Submit
. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Selecting Email Notifications
Modifying Your Client Centre News Update Preferences
If youâve already subscribed to email updates and want to update your preferences:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Client Centre Login
Select your name, then select
Update your Email Preferences
. See Figure 19.
Figure 19: Update your Email Preferences
Enter your email address, then select
Submit
. See Figure 20.
Figure 20: Submitting Request to Update Email Preferences
Open the email you received from
clientcentre@central1.com
, then select
Update email Preferences
. See Figure 21.
Figure 21: Email Containing Update Email Preferences Link
Update your preferences on the webpage that opens in your browser, then select
Save Preferences
. See Figure 22.
Figure 22: Updating Email Preferences
2.8	Accessing Forms
If the Payment Solutions Manual is open, select
Document Library
on the left. The Document Library is organized into either National Forms or BC Forms. See Figure 23. The introductory chapter of each Part also includes a Document Library section that lists all forms associated with that product or service.
Figure 23: Document Library
Alternatively, go to the Client Centreâs Forms & Manuals page to search for forms:
Select
Forms
& Manuals
from the Client Centre navigation menu, then select
Forms Search
. See Figure 24.
Figure 24: Select Forms Search
Enter a keyword, the form name, or the form number in the search field, then select
Search
. See Figure 25.
Figure 25: Searching for Forms
View your search results. Theyâre sorted by the date the form was last modified or created. See Figure 26.
Figure 26: Search Results
2.9	Unlocked Forms
Forms Between You and Your Customers
Most Payments forms that are used between you and your customers (i.e., those that arenât returned to Central 1) are  provided to you in an unlocked format. This allows you to customize the form or load it into your banking system or learning management system.
You are responsible for the following:
Having any changes you make to a form reviewed for compliance (where applicable). For example, Form 1696, Payorâs PAD Agreement complies with CPA Rule H1, Pre-Authorized Debits. You must ensure that it remains compliant.
Monitoring the Client Centre for changes to these forms and updating them in your systems immediately. Refer to Section 2.7, Getting Payment Solutions Manual Updates, for information on receiving alerts.
To identify which Payments forms are unlocked on the Client Centre:
Go to the Document Library in the Payment Solutions Manual. Unlocked forms are identified in the Format column. See Figure 27.
Figure 27: Document Library
Alternatively, open the document library for a specific Part. Unlocked forms are identified in the Format column. See Figure 28.
Figure 28: Document Library for Specific Part
Forms Between You and Central 1
Forms that are returned to Central 1 are published in a locked state. This is to protect the integrity of these forms. It is very important that these forms not be altered so that Central 1 can process your requests efficiently.
If you plan only to import the form into your banking system or learning management system, Central 1 will typically provide an unlocked version if you:
Agree that the form is required only for import into an internal system.
Agree not to edit the form (including branding). If the form is changed in any way, Central 1 cannot accept it and you must resubmit the request using the authorized version.
Test the output of the form generated from your system, if applicable, with Central 1. Upon confirmation that the form has not been altered, you can begin using the form for requests to Central 1.
Monitor Client Centre for changes to these forms and update them in your systems immediately. Refer to Section 2.7, Getting Payment Solutions Manual Updates, for information on receiving alerts.
To request an unlocked form from Central 1:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 29.
Note: 	Youâll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itâs your first login to the Client Centre, youâve deleted your cookies, or youâre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 29: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
. See Figure 30.
Figure 30: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
ServiceNow
. See Figure 31.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youâve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under âMy Favouritesâ.
Figure 31: Accessing ServiceNow
Select
Service Catalogue
. See Figure 32.
Figure 32: Select âService Catalogueâ
Select the
Client Support Services
category and then
Ask a Question
. See Figure 33.
Figure 33: Select Ask a Question
Enter the request details and select
Submit
. The request is sent to Central 1 and a ServiceNow ticket number is created. See Figure 34.
Figure 34: Ask a Question
2.10	Contact Us
Direct enquiries on Payments products and services to:
Central 1, Client Support Services
Email:
support@central1.com
Phone:
1-888-889-7878
, Option 1
Each Part contains a âContacts, Websites, and Other Resources Sectionâ in Chapter 1, which provides additional contact information specific to the product or service.
END
